topics.title: Triangle
topics.summary: Scalene, isosceles and equilateral triangles — area, perimeter and special properties.

cards.title: Scalene triangle
formulas: Area = ½ bc sinA = ½ ab sinC = ½ ac sinB

cards.title: Equilateral triangle
steps: Side increased by 2 units: new area − old area = given increase → solve for a
Equate equilateral area to Heron's area of scalene triangle to find unknown side
examples: Q1 (Practice): Side increased by 2 → area ↑ by 3+√3 → side = 1 unit
Q3 (Practice): Equilateral area = area of (50,70,80) triangle → x ≈ 63.2 m

cards.title: Isosceles triangle
steps: Altitude = 12 cm, perimeter = 48 cm → equal sides = (48−base)/2
Use altitude to split into two right triangles → find base
Area = ½ × base × altitude
examples: Altitude=12, perimeter=48 → equal side=15, base=18 → Area = ½×18×12 = 108 cm²
Equal side : base = 3:5, Area=80√11 → a=4 → Perimeter=22a=88 cm

cards.title: Triangle with inscribed circle
steps: Tangent segments from external point are equal
If BD=8, DC=6, radius=4 → let AF=AE=a
AB = a+8, AC = a+6; use Heron's on (a+8, a+6, 14) with r=4
Area = r × s → solve for a
examples: a=7 → AB=15 cm, AC=13 cm
Quarter circle R with inscribed circle r: R = r(√2+1) → R:r = (√2+1):1

topics.title: Quadrilateral & Square
topics.summary: Square, rectangle, parallelogram, rhombus, kite, trapezium and general quadrilateral.

cards.title: Square
steps: Square inscribed inside square: going one level in → side ÷ √2
Ratio largest:smallest (2 levels) = a² : (a/2)² = 4:1
Triple area → new a = a√3 → new diagonal = a√3 × √2 = a√6
examples: Diagonal of square = 8 cm → area of square with 3× area → diagonal = 8√6 cm
Man walks diagonally in 5 min at 3 km/h → diagonal=250 m → area=31250 m²
Largest:smallest square ratio = 4:1 (Q28 Practice)
Largest square in semicircle (r=5): a²+a²/4=r² → a²=20 cm²

cards.title: Rectangle
steps: l:b = 3:2, perimeter=730 → 10x=730 → x=73 → Area = 6x² = 31974 cm²
examples: l:b=3:2, perimeter=730 cm → Area=31,974 cm² (Q4 Digital Notes)
Diagonal=53 cm, area=1260 cm² → Perimeter=146 cm (Q5 Digital Notes)
Diagonal=53 cm, area=1260 cm² → cost of fencing @₹25/cm = ₹3650 (Q20 Practice)

cards.title: Rhombus
formulas: If d₁+d₂ = L and perimeter = 4P → Area = ¼(L²−4P²)
steps: (d₁+d₂)²=L² → d₁²+d₂²+2d₁d₂=L²
d₁²+d₂²=4P² (from side=P)
→ ½d₁d₂ = (L²−4P²)/4
examples: Sum of diagonals=L, perimeter=4P → Area = ¼(L²−4P²)
Smaller diagonal = side = 4 cm → larger diagonal=4√3 cm → equilateral triangle area=12√3 cm² (Q27 Practice)

cards.title: General quadrilateral (area)
examples: BD=15, perp=5 and 7 → Area = ½×15×12 = 90 cm² (Q11 Practice)

topics.title: Circle & Parts
topics.summary: Full circle, semicircle, sector, segment, arc, concentric circles and intersecting circles.

cards.title: Sector and arc
steps: Given area=209, perimeter=63, radius integer → 2r+arc=63, (θ/360)×πr²=209 → r=11, θ≈49.5°
Minute hand sweeps 270° in 45 min → Area=(270/360)×π×20² = 6600/7 cm²
examples: r=14, area of sector=231 → θ=135°
r=14, 30 min → half circle arc = (22/7)×14 = 44 cm
Q4 (Practice): r=11 (integer), θ≈49.5°
Q5 (Practice): r=20, 45 min → Area=6600/7 cm², perimeter=940/7 cm

cards.title: Segment of a circle
examples: r=42, arc=44 cm → θ = (44×360)/(2π×42) → then compute segment (Q10 Practice)

cards.title: Concentric circles
formulas: Radii in AP: middle circumference = AP mean of smallest and largest
examples: Radii 4:5:7 → inner ring area: outer ring area = π(25−16):π(49−25) = 9:24 = 3:8
Smallest circ=6π, largest=14π → middle = (6π+14π)/2 = 10π (Q in Digital Notes)
Radius decreased 10% → area change = −10−10+(100/100) = −19%

cards.title: Intersecting circles
steps: Common chord = 7, radius = 7 → triangle is equilateral → θ=60°
examples: r=7, chord=7 → Common area = 98(π/6−√3/4)
r=14, chord=14 → Common area = 392(π/6−√3/4) (Q36 Practice)

cards.title: Square inscribed in semicircle
examples: r=5 → a²=20 cm² (Digital Notes)
r=15 → a²=180 cm² (Q18 Practice)

topics.title: Regular Hexagon
topics.summary: Area, perimeter and triangle relationships for regular hexagon.

cards.title: Regular hexagon formulas
formulas: Area = (3√3/2) s²  where s = side length

cards.title: Square vs hexagon (same circumscribed circle)
formulas: Square inscribed in circle r: Area = 2r²
Regular hexagon inscribed in circle r: Area = (3√3/2)r²

cards.title: Triangle PQR from midpoints of hexagon sides
examples: Hexagon side=12 cm, PQR joins midpoints of AB, CD, EF → Area=81√3 cm²

cards.title: Hexagon with equilateral triangle extensions
examples: Hexagon side=6, 4 equilateral triangles (APF, QAB, DCR, DES) → shaded area = 72√3 cm²

topics.title: Path & Border Problems
topics.summary: Paths around rectangles, circles and squares — inside or outside.

cards.title: Path outside rectangle
formulas: Path area = Outer area − Inner area
examples: 14×7 lawn, path width=2 outside + cross path 1m inside → cost@₹25 = ₹3000 (Q34 Practice)

cards.title: Path around circle
examples: Circular garden diameter=10, path=1m → % increase = (6²−5²)/5² ×100 = 44%
Pond r≈14 m (total area 804.25, pond alone 615.75) (Q8 Practice)

cards.title: Cross roads inside square park
steps: Two roads of width w parallel to sides of square with side a
Road area = 2×a×w − w² (subtract intersection once)
examples: Square 22m, road width 2m → 22×2+22×2−4 = 84 m² → cost@₹100 = ₹8400

cards.title: Horses grazing in square park
steps: Each horse at adjacent corner with rope length r → grazes quarter circle
Ungrazed area = total area − 2×(πr²/4)
examples: Square 15m, rope=7m → ungrazed = 225 − 2×(22/7×49/4) = 225−77 = 148 m² (Q17 Practice)

topics.title: String Around Circles
topics.summary: Length of string tightly wound around n touching circles of diameter d.

cards.title: String length formula by circle count
cards.tip: The 2πr is always constant (one full circle worth of wrap). Add n×d for n circles in a row.

cards.title: Three touching circles
examples: r=6 → 12π+36 cm (Digital Notes)
r=9 → 18π+54 cm (Q7 Practice)

topics.title: Composite Shapes
topics.summary: Shapes formed by combining rectangles, triangles, semicircles, and quadrants.

cards.title: Rectangle + semicircle + triangle (field)
steps: Total area = rectangle area + triangle area + semicircle area
Rectangle: 25×18=450, Triangle: ½×12×15=90, Semicircle: ½π×21²=693
Total = 1233... check with answer

cards.title: Rectangle with semicircles on sides
steps: Length = 2×breadth (given)
Add two semicircles on shorter sides (radius=b/2) and two on longer sides (radius=l/2)
Total area = l×b + 2×(π(b/2)²/2) + 2×(π(l/2)²/2)

cards.title: Semicircles on triangle sides (Q35 Practice)
steps: Draw semicircles outward on each side of a right triangle
Shaded area = area of semicircle on hypotenuse − area of triangle
examples: AB=21, BC=28, AC=35 (right triangle) → Shaded = ½π(17.5)² − ½×21×28 = 294 cm²

cards.title: Triangle at each vertex with circles
steps: Sum of angles of triangle = 180° → sectors sum to one full circle
Area excl. circles = Triangle area − πr²

cards.title: Wire bent to square then circle
steps: Perimeter of square = 4a
Same wire = circumference of circle → 2πr = 4a
Area of circle = πr²
examples: Area of square=169 → a=13 → perimeter=52 → r=26/π → area=π×(26/π)² = 215 cm²

topics.title: Cost Problems
topics.summary: Find cost after % change in dimensions. Multiply dimension change factors.

cards.title: Carpet cost with % dimension change
steps: New length = old length × (1+40/100), New breadth = old breadth × (1+25/100)
New area = New length × New breadth
Cost = New area × rate per m²

cards.title: Roller / playground area
formulas: Area covered per revolution = 2πr × length of roller
Total area = revolutions × area per revolution
examples: Diameter=35cm, length=100cm, 200 revolutions → 200×(22/7)×17.5×100 = 11,00,000 cm² = 110 m²

topics.title: Octagon & Special
topics.summary: Regular octagon from rectangle and special inscribed figure problems.

cards.title: Octagon from rectangle (cut corners)
steps: Rectangle l=8, b=6. Cut isosceles right triangles from corners
Let cut length = x at each corner
Octagon side = x√2 (the cut hypotenuse) or (remaining side)
All 8 sides equal → solve: b−2x = x√2
examples: Q26 (Practice): l=8, b=6 → side = 3(√11)−7 cm

cards.title: Square and hexagon in same circle
